name: mypreview
description: Bring the modern, agentic IDE browser experience directly into VS Code with CDP-backed live preview and element picking.

commands.title: My Preview: Pin Current Browser Panel
My Preview: Open In External Browser
My Preview: Copy Context For AI
My Docs: Search Docs Or Web
My Docs: Search In Specific Docs
My Preview: Browser Bridge & MCP Setup

myDocs.searchEndpoint.description: Search API endpoint. The extension sends q, sourceName, and sourceBaseUrl query parameters by default.

myDocs.apiKey.description: Optional API key. Sent as both x-api-key and Bearer auth so it is easy to adapt to a real provider.

myDocs.defaultDocs.description: Named documentation sources used by myDocs.searchInDocs and Docs Chat.

name.description: Human-friendly label shown in the UI.

baseUrl.description: Base URL or origin for the documentation set.

myPreview.browserExecutablePath.description: Optional explicit path to Chrome, Chromium, or Edge used for the CDP-backed browser workbench.

myPreview.allowLocalhost.description: Allow localhost and loopback URLs in the browser workbench.

myPreview.allowPrivateHosts.description: Allow RFC1918/private-network hosts in the browser workbench.

myPreview.allowedHosts.description: Optional allowlist of hostnames. When set, only these hosts can be opened in the browser workbench.

myPreview.viewportWidth.description: Viewport width used by the headless browser for screenshots and hit testing.

myPreview.viewportHeight.description: Viewport height used by the headless browser for screenshots and hit testing.

myPreview.screenshotFormat.description: Screenshot encoding for the workbench. JPEG is much faster and uses less memory than PNG at typical viewport sizes.

myPreview.jpegQuality.description: JPEG quality (40–100) when screenshotFormat is jpeg. Lower values are faster and smaller.

myPreview.maxOuterHtmlLength.description: Maximum sanitized outerHTML length included in copied AI context.

myPreview.enableBrowserBridge.description: When true, starts a localhost HTTP bridge so MCP and other tools can control the browser (see browser-bridge.json in extension global storage).

myPreview.browserBridgePort.description: HTTP port for the browser bridge (0 = random free port). Set a fixed port if you reference it from MCP config.

myPreview.browserBridgeToken.description: Optional shared secret for the bridge (sent as X-Browser-Bridge-Token). Leave empty to auto-generate and persist in global state.

myPreview.persistUserDataDir.description: When true, uses a persistent browser profile directory to preserve cookies and localStorage across sessions. When false (default), uses temporary profiles to avoid Chrome singleton lock conflicts.

myPreview.debugLogging.description: Write Browser Workbench navigation, capture, and webview diagnostics to the My Preview output channel.
